usageTrace.validationUse: RL_SEQUENCE_ORDER_NOT_CONTIGUOUS: requires the SET of SequenceOrder values across all slots to be unique and exactly {1..N}; does not check JSON array position agreement, does not tie a specific ordinal to a specific Role
usageTrace.bundleUse: republished verbatim as part of the RUN_LAYOUT artifact in every release; not special-cased by CatalogBundleAssembler
usageTrace.runtimeBehaviorUse: none found; no Process A code branches on a specific SequenceOrder value, only on its shape

distinguishActualVsIntended: The contiguity check is real and implemented, but validates shape only (a permutation of 1..N), never a specific role-to-ordinal mapping. No test, validator, or bundle-assembly logic anywhere asserts a specific role must hold a specific ordinal.

orderingAlreadyRepresented.arrayListOrder: yes, currently consistent with sequenceOrder values, but not schema-enforced to match
orderingAlreadyRepresented.slotIndex: yes, structurally available, unused by any consumer
orderingAlreadyRepresented.dayPreferenceOrExplicitOrdinal: no separate field; SequenceOrder is the only explicit ordinal
orderingAlreadyRepresented.conclusion: SequenceOrder is redundant with array order under current usage

affects.bundleHash: yes, indirectly, as a mechanical consequence of being a required serialized field
affects.documentationOnly: yes, beyond the shape check, functions only as an authoring convention

necessityAnalysis.processBSchedulingEvidence: Golden Fixture v3 assigns scheduledDate/weekday per day independently (e.g. 2026-08-04); brief explicitly forbids catalog-level weekday assignment; no code path connects SequenceOrder to scheduledDate

canTwoRepresentationsDisagree.explanation: Nothing enforces array position matches sequenceOrder value. A reordering that keeps the value SET {1,2,3,4} but assigns them to different array positions/roles would pass RL_SEQUENCE_ORDER_NOT_CONTIGUOUS silently.
canTwoRepresentationsDisagree.currentArtifactStatus: currently consistent (no live disagreement), but the representation-disagreement risk is unguarded

ifRemovedWhatIsLost: RL_SEQUENCE_ORDER_NOT_CONTIGUOUS would need to be removed or re-based on array index (mechanical schema change)
no domain/training information lost -- no consumer reads the value for behavioral purposes
authoring convenience of a self-describing field independent of array position (minor ergonomic property only)

classification.rationale: No coaching/scheduling authority determines which numeric slot holds which role; per AUD-017's own recorded reasoning, the brief mandates shape only, not order.

candidateOutcomes.fit: does not resolve redundancy, only renames it
not supported -- Process A authoring-shape concern; Process B already owns real scheduling separately
viable, a stricter version of DERIVE_FROM_COLLECTION_ORDER

finalStatus: D1 usage trace and necessity analysis complete.
